focus: Alpine family hotel group, minimal filings — exercises footprint-based revenue (rooms x occupancy x ADR), seasonal FTE handling, succession ambiguity, location moat vs lease/capex exposure.
rawData:
FIRMENBUCH (Austria, retrieved June 2026):
Hotel Edelweiss Betriebs GmbH, FN 23456k, seat: Saalbach-Hinterglemm, Salzburg. Founded 1978 (taken over from predecessor KG in 1996).
Share capital EUR 36,336. Shareholders: Alois Hofer (50%), Brigitte Hofer (50%).
Managing directors: Alois Hofer (born 1954), Brigitte Hofer (born 1957), both since 1996. No further Prokura. No other registered group companies found, but the land register shows the hotel properties are owned by 'Hofer Immobilien GmbH & Co KG' (same family), and leased to the Betriebs GmbH.

FILED ACCOUNTS (micro/small regime, abridged — no P&L):
FY2024: Total assets EUR 4.1m; Fixed assets EUR 2.6m (mainly fixtures, furniture, a 2023 wellness refurbishment capitalised at EUR 1.9m); Equity EUR 0.9m (22%); Liabilities to banks EUR 1.7m; Liabilities to shareholders EUR 0.8m. Average employees: 92.
FY2023: Total assets EUR 4.4m; Equity EUR 1.1m (25%); Average employees: 88.
FY2022: Total assets EUR 3.2m; Equity EUR 1.3m (40.6%); Average employees: 85.

LAND REGISTER (on the Immobilien KG's properties): Höchstbetragshypothek EUR 6.0m, Raiffeisenverband Salzburg, registered November 2023 (replacing a 2016 mortgage of EUR 3.5m which was deleted at the same time).

WEB PRESENCE: Three properties marketed as 'Edelweiss Group': Hotel Edelweiss (4*S, 78 rooms, Saalbach), Berghotel Alpenrose (4*, 52 rooms, Hinterglemm), Chalet-Dorf Bergzeit (14 chalets, opened December 2023). Booking.com scores: 8.9 (1,840 reviews), 8.6 (1,120 reviews), 9.4 (210 reviews). Summer and winter season operation; closed November and post-Easter to mid-May. Website careers page (June 2026): 11 open roles for winter season 2026/27 incl. chef de partie, receptionist, spa therapist; 'staff house with 40 single rooms' advertised as a benefit.

PRESS: Salzburger Nachrichten, February 2024: 'Edelweiss invests EUR 8m in the new Chalet-Dorf and wellness expansion — the largest investment in the company's history. Alois Hofer: "Our children both have careers in Vienna; we will see what the future brings. One does not build something like this to stop."'
Hotel & Touristik trade magazine, 2025 ranking: Saalbach-Hinterglemm average 4-star winter ADR EUR 210-260, occupancy ~78% winter / ~55% summer; season length ~200 operating days.
humanReviewHints: Revenue: footprint method (130 rooms + 14 chalets, ~200 operating days, blended occupancy ~65-70%, ADR 210-260 winter) should land around EUR 9-13m; headcount method (92 seasonal avg employees x EUR 60-110k) overlaps — engine must convert seasonal heads to FTE reasoning and reconcile.

Critical structure point: OpCo/PropCo split — the GmbH being analysed does NOT own the hotels; the EUR 6m mortgage sits on the family PropCo. Engine must catch that the operating company's filed balance sheet understates group leverage and that any deal must address the lease.

Health: equity ratio fell 41% -> 22% across the big capex; mortgage nearly doubled Nov 2023; shareholder loans injected. Stretched but explainable by the investment — verdict should be nuanced, not 'distress'.

Owner motivation: both owners ~70, children explicitly 'in Vienna careers', yet record investment 2023 — contradictory signals; a good dossier names the contradiction and still commits (e.g. external sale more likely than family succession; investment made to keep asset saleable/competitive).
Moat: location/destination + staff housing in a labour-scarce resort market. Deal-killers: lease terms with family PropCo, key-couple dependence, seasonal labour.
